question: Is a fireworks display visible in the night sky above the cityscape?
Are there at least 5 distinct firework bursts visible in the sky?
Do the fireworks display varied colors (e.g., gold, red, green, blue, purple) rather than a single monochrome tone?
Is at least one chrysanthemum-style firework burst (dense spherical bloom) clearly distinguishable?
Is at least one willow-style firework burst (long trailing arcs drooping downward) clearly distinguishable?
Is at least one ring-formation firework burst clearly distinguishable?
Are colorful reflections of the fireworks visible on the river surface?
Do the reflections on the water vertically align with the specific firework bursts above them?
Do the colors of the reflections on the river match the colors of the corresponding firework bursts?
Do the reflections exhibit a realistic water texture (rippled/elongated streaks) rather than appearing as sharp mirror images?
Are the fireworks positioned naturally in the sky without overlapping the buildings in the foreground?
Are the original skyline buildings and their structural details completely preserved?
Are the original city lights and their existing reflections on the water preserved?
Is the sky free from unnatural banding, blocky artifacts, or repetitive 'copy-paste' patterns?
Are the edges of the fireworks and smoke blends smooth, without harsh halos or cutout lines?
